name: Ratri
description: Ratri, the Hindu goddess of night, is a powerful and enigmatic figure embodying the darkness, stillness, and potential of the nocturnal realm. She is described as clothing the world in darkness, bringing rest and respite to all living beings after the activities of the day. Ratri is often invoked for protection against the dangers and fears associated with the night, including thieves, predators, and negative energies. Her origins are ancient, appearing prominently in the Rigveda, where hymns are dedicated to her, often in conjunction with Ushas, the goddess of dawn. These hymns portray a cyclical relationship between darkness and light, emphasizing their interdependence and the necessity of both for the cosmic balance. Ratri is not merely the absence of light but an active force, a cosmic womb from which new beginnings emerge. She is seen as a guardian, offering solace and security during the vulnerable hours of darkness. While not as widely worshipped as some other Hindu deities, Ratri holds a significant place in understanding the Hindu worldview, representing the cyclical nature of time, the importance of rest and introspection, and the power inherent in the unknown. She embodies the feminine principle in its darker, more mysterious aspect, a counterpoint to the solar and active energies of other deities. Her presence reminds us that darkness is not inherently evil but a necessary part of life's rhythm, offering a space for healing, rejuvenation, and the incubation of new possibilities. She is often associated with deep sleep, dreams, and the subconscious mind.
subtitle: Goddess of Night and Darkness

keyMyths.title: Ratri Sukta
keyMyths.description: The Ratri Sukta in the Rigveda praises Ratri, seeking her protection from darkness and its associated perils. It highlights her role as a guardian and provider of rest.

keyMyths.title: Ratri and Ushas Dialogue
keyMyths.description: Hymns often depict Ratri and Ushas as sisters or complementary forces, highlighting the cyclical nature of day and night. This emphasizes the balance between darkness and light in the universe.

keyMyths.title: Ratri's Protection
keyMyths.description: Devotees invoke Ratri for protection against dangers lurking in the night, including thieves, wild animals, and evil spirits. She provides solace and security during the vulnerable hours.

sources.author: Wendy Doniger O'Flaherty
